Badger Grapple Red — v20.9 Audio System

ROOT CAUSE FIRST: the game shipped completely silent with `audio:
{noAudio:true}` hardcoded in the Phaser config, every playSfx() call a
no-op stub, and — the real tell — a global window error handler that
explicitly swallowed any error whose message contained "audio device",
"AudioContext", or "sound". That's not a stylistic choice, that's someone
hitting a real crash (almost certainly the standard "AudioContext was not
allowed to start" autoplay-policy error every mobile browser throws) and
hiding the symptom rather than fixing it. Also found and removed a second
piece of dead/backwards code from the same abandoned attempt: `unlockSfx()`
was literally setting a readiness flag to false on "unlock."

WHAT WAS BUILT: a standalone Web Audio system (src/systems/audio.js) that
deliberately never touches Phaser's own sound manager — `audio:
{noAudio:true}` stays exactly as it was, correctly, since nothing in the
engine uses Phaser audio anymore. Instead:
- The AudioContext is created lazily, only from a real user gesture
  (every button press already calls unlockAudio() first), and explicitly
  .resume()'d if suspended — which is the actual fix for the autoplay
  error that got hidden before, not a workaround for it.
- Every sound-producing function is individually wrapped so a failure
  can never crash the game or reach the boot-error screen — defensive
  at the source, not via a blanket window-level suppression.
- Because the new system doesn't need that blanket suppression, it's
  been removed from main.js. This is a real behavior change: audio-
  related errors are no longer silently hidden. If something does go
  wrong with sound going forward, it'll surface instead of vanishing
  the way it did before.
- All SFX (step, bump, talk, door, menu open/move, hit, miss, win, lose,
  badge fanfare) and three looping music beds (title/overworld/battle)
  are synthesized with raw oscillators and noise buffers — no audio
  files, nothing to fail to load.
- Real mute toggle in the Options menu, persisted to save state.

VERIFICATION — this one needed real functional proof, not just "no
console errors," since the whole point is sound actually happening:
- Instrumented AudioContext.prototype.createOscillator/createBufferSource
  in a live headless browser to COUNT real sound-node creation, not just
  check for crashes. Confirmed: title tap produces sound events, walking
  produces one event per step, a 4-turn battle produces dozens of hit/
  miss/music events, all with zero thrown errors.
- Verified the mute toggle actually flips and persists (caught my own
  test mistake along the way: the coach's welcome message blocks the
  menu button on first spawn by design — my first test attempt was
  silently dismissing that dialogue instead of opening the menu, not a
  real bug. Corrected the test, then confirmed the toggle genuinely
  works: audioMuted true -> false -> true across repeated toggles).

Cache: ?v=209. Proof: title/note reads "v20.9 Audio System".
